reasoning: The Bank Term Funding Program (BTFP) ceased issuing new loans on March 11, 2024. Since the maximum loan term was one year, all outstanding loans matured by mid-March 2025. FRED data clearly shows that the series value dropped to exactly 0.0m USD in March 2025 and has remained at exactly 0.0 for over 40 consecutive weeks since then. Given that the program is permanently shuttered and all balances have been settled for months, there is no credible mechanism by which the loan amount could spontaneously increase above its Jan 18, 2026 baseline (of 0.0) by January 25 or February 17, 2026. The only non-zero probability stems from black-swan events, such as the Federal Reserve unexpectedly resurrecting the exact same BTFP program under the exact same FRED series ID within the next month due to a sudden banking crisis. Therefore, probabilities are set to the minimum allowable threshold for high confidence, with a tiny increment for the later date in accordance with time-horizon uncertainty principles.
